summary: Replace the rust-diffforge MCP placeholder with a local MCP registry UI for adding server configs and selecting agent/workspace access.

review_items.summary: The MCPs rail item opens an operational local settings surface instead of a placeholder.
Users can create and edit local MCP definitions for stdio command, HTTP URL, SSE URL, or pasted JSON config modes.
Each saved MCP can be scoped to selected local agents and selected synced workspaces through local UI state.
A built-in Agent Coordinator MCP appears in the registry as planned and disabled until the runtime implementation exists.
This change stores local configuration only and does not start MCP processes, connect to user MCP URLs, or expose MCPs to agents yet.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, or server data access change is required.

rules: The MCP tab must not execute user-supplied commands or contact user-supplied URLs until a separate MCP runtime proposal is accepted.
The Agent Coordinator MCP must default to disabled and remain non-toggleable until its runtime behavior exists.
Local MCP definitions may include command, arguments, URL, headers, environment text, and JSON config text, but the UI must present them as saved configuration rather than connected servers.
Agent and workspace access selections are local routing intent only; they must not imply that Codex, Claude Code, or workspace terminals already consume MCP configuration.
Saved MCP config must remain local desktop state and must not be sent to the backend by this change.

patches.summary: Implement MCP registry state, editor, access controls, Agent Coordinator placeholder, and local persistence.

compatibility.notes: The change is limited to authenticated desktop UI and localStorage-backed settings.
